seoTitle: How to Use Azure Maps in Power BI Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to add the Azure Maps visual in Power BI, plot location data with bubbles or heat maps, and customize map themes.
intro: Plotting logistics or regional sales data on a map shouldn't mean fighting slow rendering or inaccurate placement. This guide shows how to add Power BI's Azure Maps visual, assign location fields correctly, and switch between bubble layers, heat maps, and different visual themes. You'll also learn how to connect a slicer so the map pans responsively based on user selections.

steps.heading: Verify your location data
steps.body: Open your report and confirm your location columns are correctly categorized under data properties before adding the visual.

steps.heading: Add the Azure Maps visual
steps.body: In the Visualizations pane, click the Azure Maps tile icon to drop an empty map frame onto your canvas.

steps.heading: Map your location fields
steps.body: Drag location names or precise latitude and longitude fields into the designated map data buckets.

steps.heading: Choose a visual layer
steps.body: Toggle Bubbles for standard data points with size and color driven by your metrics, or switch to Heat Map to analyze density clusters instead.

steps.heading: Style and connect a slicer
steps.body: Adjust the map Style to satellite, hybrid, or high contrast, enable zoom and compass controls, then connect a slicer so the map responds to filtering.

faq.question: What location data does Azure Maps in Power BI require?
faq.answer: You can use location names, postal codes, or precise latitude and longitude fields, though exact coordinates give the most accurate placement.

faq.question: Can I switch between bubbles and a heat map?
faq.answer: Yes, you can toggle off bubbles and activate the Heat Map layer instead to analyze density clusters across your data.

faq.question: Does the map update when I click a slicer?
faq.answer: Yes, connecting a standard slicer to your report lets the map zoom and pan responsively as users click through regional fields.

faq.question: What background styles are available for the map?
faq.answer: You can switch between satellite imagery, dark canvas mode, high contrast street views, and standard hybrid layouts.
